revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:2 nguŏk 24 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:2月24日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 2월 24일]][[zh-min-nan:2 goe̍h 24 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:二月廿四]][[zh:二月廿四]]
{{Not to be confused with|February 24|qq=February 24}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=二月廿四|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 2월 24일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar February|qq=}} '''February 24''' is the 24th day of the month {{ill|February (Lunisolar)|ja|2月 (旧暦)|lt=February (Kisaragi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 2nd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senshū|qid=Q96759839|1=senshū}}{{efn|ja|先勝|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9418169}}. == Events == * {{ill|Genkō (1331–1334)|en|Genkō (1331–1334)|qid=Q1194556|lt=Genkō|1=Genkō (1331–1334)}} 3rd year / {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|qid=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō|1=Jōkyō}} 2nd year (leap February) ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1333|en|1333|qid=Q6004|lt=1333|1=1333}} {{ill|April 9|en|April 9|qid=Q2504|lt=April 9|1=April 9}}) – Exiled by the Kamakura shogunate, {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|qid=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}} escaped from Oki Province and set out for Kyoto. * {{ill|Eitoku|en|Eitoku|qid=Q1145805|lt=Eitoku|1=Eitoku}} 1st year / {{ill|Kōwa (Muromachi period)|en|Kōwa (Muromachi period)|qid=Q1145818|lt=Kōwa|1=Kōwa (Muromachi period)}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1381|en|1381|qid=Q6331|lt=1381|1=1381}} {{ill|March 20|en|March 20|qid=Q2427|lt=March 20|1=March 20}}) – Due to the 【辛酉】 Revolution ({{ill|Xinyou Revolution|ja|1=Yin Metal Rooster|2=ja|辛酉|zh|1=Yin Metal Rooster|2=ja|辛酉|ko|신유|lt_ko=신유|fr|Coq de métal|de|Metall-Hahn|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xinyou Revolution|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), the Northern Court changed its era name from {{ill|Kōryaku|en|Kōryaku|qid=Q252777|lt=Kōryaku|1=Kōryaku}} to {{ill|Eitoku|en|Eitoku|qid=Q1145805|lt=Eitoku|1=Eitoku}}. * {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|qid=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 14th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1843|en|1843|qid=Q7629|lt=1843|1=1843}} {{ill|March 24|en|March 24|qid=Q2453|lt=March 24|1=March 24}}) – In a political maneuver, the Nanto bugyō, {{ill|Torii Chūyō|ja|鳥居忠耀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii Chūyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居耀蔵|1=Torii Yōzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10729521}}, orchestrated a scheme that resulted in the Hokumachi bugyō, {{ill|Tōyama Kagemoto|en|Tōyama Kagemoto|qid=Q7862747|lt=Toyama Kagemoto|1=Tōyama Kagemoto}}, being demoted to the minor post of {{ill|Ōmotsuke|ja|大目附|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmotsuke|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大目付|1=Ōmetsuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11438061}}. * {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|qid=Q1327259|lt=Ansei|1=Ansei}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1854|en|1854|qid=Q7670|lt=1854|1=1854}} {{ill|March 22|en|March 22|qid=Q2435|lt=March 22|1=March 22}}) – Commodore Perry’s expedition conducted an experiment with a wired {{ill|Electrical telegraph|en|Electrical telegraph|qid=Q6501061|lt=telegraph|1=Electrical telegraph}} apparatus in Yokohama, marking Japan’s first venture into electrical communication. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1868|en|1868|qid=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}} {{ill|March 17|en|March 17|qid=Q2419|lt=March 17|1=March 17}}) – The Western scholar Yanagihara Shunsan founded Japan’s first newspaper, the {{ill|Chūgai Shimbun|ja|中外新聞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūgai Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, through a Japanese editorial effort. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1869|en|1869|qid=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|qid=Q2507|lt=April 5|1=April 5}}) – During the Tokyo period of the Emperor’s residence, it was decided that the {{ill|Daijō-kan|en|Daijō-kan|qid=Q1192939|lt=Daijō-kan|1=Daijō-kan}} would also be located in Tokyo, formalizing the decision for Tokyo as the new capital. == Births == * {{ill|Shōxīng (Song)|ja|紹興|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōxīng (Song)|1=Shōxīng (Song)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1306263}} 5th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1139|en|1139|qid=Q19608|lt=1139|1=1139}} {{ill|March 26|en|March 26|qid=Q2456|lt=March 26|1=March 26}}) – {{ill|Lu Jiuyuan|en|Lu Jiuyuan|qid=Q2466384|lt=Rikusōzan|1=Lu Jiuyuan}}, a Confucian scholar of the Southern Song (died {{ill|1192|en|1192|qid=Q19739|lt=1192|1=1192}}). * {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|qid=Q1327259|lt=Ansei|1=Ansei}} 7th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1860|en|1860|qid=Q7691|lt=1860|1=1860}} {{ill|March 16|en|March 16|qid=Q2417|lt=March 16|1=March 16}}) – {{ill|Ōyama Sutematsu|en|Ōyama Sutematsu|qid=Q3594061|lt=Ōyama Sutematsu|1=Ōyama Sutematsu}}, an educator and the wife of Ōyama Iwao (died {{ill|1919|en|1919|qid=Q2157|lt=1919|1=1919}}). == Deaths == * {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|qid=Q1145262|lt=Hōei|1=Hōei}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1704|en|1704|qid=Q6824|lt=1704|1=1704}} {{ill|March 29|en|March 29|qid=Q2459|lt=March 29|1=March 29}}) – {{ill|Naitō Jōsō|en|Naitō Jōsō|qid=Q3335265|lt=Naitō Jōsō|1=Naitō Jōsō}}, a haiku poet and one of the group known as Shōmon Jūtetsu (born {{ill|1662|en|1662|qid=Q7574|lt=1662|1=1662}}). * {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 15th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1610|en|1610|qid=Q6765|lt=1610|1=1610}} {{ill|March 19|en|March 19|qid=Q2424|lt=March 19|1=March 19}}) – {{ill|Hasegawa Tōhaku|en|Hasegawa Tōhaku|qid=Q719668|lt=Hasegawa Tōhakubō|1=Hasegawa Tōhaku}}, a painter and the founder of the Hasegawa school (born {{ill|1539|en|1539|qid=Q6479|lt=1539|1=1539}}). == Commemorations ==
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|2|23|2|25|1|24|3|2|0224|qq=}}
==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}

[[ko:음력 2월 24일]] [[ja:2月24日 (旧暦)]] [[zh:二月廿四]]
[[ar:February 24 (Lunisolar)]]
